chief_complaint: Something is wrong with my nerves and no one can tell me what.
history_of_present_illness: 4-month history of episodic neurological symptoms. Four months ago: numbness and tingling in left leg lasting 3 weeks, resolved spontaneously. Two months ago: brief episode (5 days) of blurred vision in right eye, described as looking through wax paper, with some eye pain on movement. Resolved. Ongoing: intermittent fatigue, occasional migratory joint aches (knees, wrists), memory feels foggy. Two weeks ago returned from a 2-week camping trip in upstate New York and Vermont border. Does not recall a tick bite but was in the woods constantly. Had one episode of fever and malaise after returning that resolved in 3 days.
social_history: Non-smoker. Occasional alcohol. Works as a graphic designer, recently stressed about a project deadline.
family_history: Maternal aunt with multiple sclerosis. No family history of Lyme disease or autoimmune conditions.
physical_exam: Alert, cooperative. Cranial nerves grossly intact, though patient reports right eye still feels not quite right. Motor strength 5/5 throughout. Sensation reports mild decreased sensation over left anterior thigh. Reflexes 2+ symmetric. Gait normal. Romberg negative. No meningeal signs. No rash on full skin survey. No joint effusions on exam. No lymphadenopathy.
